prescriptions.title: Respiratory Infection Treatment
prescriptions.notes: Rocky has been showing signs of coughing and nasal discharge consistent with a mild respiratory infection. Administer with food to avoid stomach upset. Monitor for improvement after 48 hours.

prescriptions.title: Seasonal Allergy Management
prescriptions.notes: Rocky has been scratching excessively and showing skin inflammation. Do not stop medication abruptly. Taper dose as instructed at follow-up. Keep away from other pets during treatment.

prescriptions.title: Post-Surgery Pain Management
prescriptions.notes: Max underwent ACL repair on the left hind leg. Administer with food. Restrict physical activity — no running or jumping for the full duration. Contact the clinic immediately if vomiting, diarrhea, or appetite loss occurs.

prescriptions.title: Hyperthyroidism Control
prescriptions.notes: Luna has been diagnosed with hyperthyroidism. Administer consistently every 12 hours, ideally at the same times each day. Wear gloves when handling the tablet. Recheck thyroid levels in 3 weeks.

prescriptions.title: Post-Dental Pain Relief
prescriptions.notes: Luna had three teeth extracted during her dental cleaning. Administer once daily for the prescribed duration. Do not use with other NSAIDs. Ensure adequate water intake.

prescriptions.title: Post-Dental Antibiotic Course
prescriptions.notes: Prescribed following dental extractions to prevent secondary infection. Administer with a small amount of food. Complete the full course even if Luna appears to have recovered.

prescriptions.title: Motion Sickness Prevention
prescriptions.notes: Mochi experiences severe motion sickness during car rides. Administer 2 hours before travel. Shake the oral liquid well before use. Store refrigerated.

prescriptions.title: IBD Flare-Up Management
prescriptions.notes: Mochi has inflammatory bowel disease. This course is to manage an active flare-up. Feed a bland diet during treatment. Gradually taper the dose over the final week — do not stop abruptly.

prescriptions.title: Chlamydiosis (Psittacosis) Treatment
prescriptions.notes: Kiwi has tested positive for Chlamydia psittaci. Administer drops directly into the beak or mix with a small amount of soft food. Keep Kiwi isolated from other birds for the full duration. Wash hands thoroughly after handling.
